indicators.name: hard wood flooring
indicators.rationale: Hard flooring can be noisy and uncomfortable for sleep.

indicators.name: exposed brick walls
indicators.rationale: Exposed brick walls can be cold and uninviting for sleep.

indicators.name: high ceilings
indicators.rationale: High ceilings can make the space feel too open and cold for sleep.

indicators.name: large windows
indicators.rationale: Large windows can let in too much light and noise, disrupting sleep.

indicators.name: open layout
indicators.rationale: Open layout with no walls or doors can lead to noise and drafts, making it unsuitable for sleep.

reasoning_summary: The indoor scene is not well-suited for sleep (primary). The hard wood flooring, exposed brick walls, high ceilings, large windows, and open layout all contribute to an environment that is too cold, noisy, and drafty for comfortable sleep.
